proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months — patients with ecog_ps=1 and ecog_ps=2 have shorter PFS than ecog_ps=0.
Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
Presence of brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: ECOG 0 = 4.33, ECOG 1 = 3.19, ECOG 2 = 2.15. Both ECOG 1 and ECOG 2 have significantly shorter PFS than ECOG 0 (ECOG 2 vs 0: -2.17 months, ECOG 1 vs 0: -1.14 months, both p < 1e-300).
Mean pfs_months 2.92 in stage_iv=1 vs 4.36 in stage_iv=0; coefficient -1.44 months, p ~ 0.
Mean pfs_months 2.74 with brain mets vs 3.66 without; coefficient -0.92 months, p ~ 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).

analyses.result_summary: Albumin coefficient +0.44 months per g/dL, p = 2.8e-109. Strongly favorable prognostic factor.
LDH coefficient -0.000511 months per U/L, p = 3.6e-5. Higher LDH is associated with shorter PFS.
Weight loss coefficient -0.073 months per percent lost, p = 7.5e-179. Strong negative prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (inflammation hypothesis).
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (typical clinical expectation).
Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than male sex.

analyses.result_summary: CRP coefficient +0.00031 months per mg/L, p = 0.78. No significant univariate association.
NLR coefficient +0.0039 months per unit, p = 0.42. No significant univariate association.
Age coefficient +0.170 months per year, p ~ 0. Counter to clinical expectation: in this cohort older patients have LONGER PFS.
sex_female coefficient -0.204 months, p = 2.4e-24. Females have SHORTER PFS in this cohort, opposite to the hypothesized direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Current smokers have shorter pfs_months than never smokers, with former smokers intermediate.
Squamous histology is associated with shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: never 3.67, former 3.61, current 2.99. Current vs never coef -0.69 (p = 7.4e-106); former vs never coef -0.07 (p = 0.019). Current smokers have markedly shorter PFS.
Mean PFS: adenocarcinoma 3.67, squamous 2.81. Squamous coefficient -0.857, p ~ 0. Squamous has substantially shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with egfr_mutation=1 have longer pfs_months than wild-type patients (univariate, before considering treatment).
Patients with kras_g12c=1 have longer pfs_months than wild-type patients (univariate; expected if sotorasib effect leaks into the marginal estimate).
Patients with alk_fusion=1 have shorter pfs_months than wild-type patients (univariate).
Patients with stk11_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than wild-type patients.
Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than wild-type patients.
Patients with tmb_high=1 have longer pfs_months than tmb_high=0 (TMB as favorable marker).
Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months (favorable marker).

analyses.result_summary: EGFR coefficient +0.189, p = 1.9e-10. EGFR-mutated patients have slightly longer PFS marginally.
KRAS G12C coefficient +0.78, p = 4.8e-151. Strong positive marginal effect (likely partly driven by sotorasib responders).
ALK fusion coefficient -0.81, p = 2.9e-69. Patients with ALK fusion have shorter PFS (no targeted therapy is in dataset).
STK11 coefficient +0.009, p = 0.75. No significant marginal effect.
BRCA2 coefficient -0.119, p = 0.041. Marginally shorter PFS in BRCA2-mutated patients.
TMB high coefficient -0.190, p = 9.1e-18. TMB high is associated with SHORTER PFS marginally — opposite to favorable-marker hypothesis. Disappears after adjustment (adj p = 0.46).
PD-L1 TPS coefficient +0.067, p = 0.16. No significant marginal association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, or bun_mg_dl are associated with shorter pfs_months (organ dysfunction).

analyses.result_summary: Hemoglobin coefficient near 0 with non-significant p in adjusted model (joint p = 0.92). No meaningful univariate effect detectable above noise of stronger covariates.

analyses.code: Repeated univariate OLS for each lab vs pfs_months.
analyses.result_summary: All routine chemistries (alkaline phosphatase, AST, ALT, total bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium) show null or near-null univariate effects (|coef| < 0.01 month/unit, p mostly > 0.1). They are not strong PFS predictors in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment with treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months (overall, unadjusted).
Treatment with treatment_sotorasib is associated with longer pfs_months (overall, unadjusted).
Treatment with treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months (overall, unadjusted).
Treatment with treatment_osimertinib is associated with longer pfs_months (overall, unadjusted).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 3.41 on pembro vs 3.45 off; coef -0.035, p = 0.081. No marginal benefit (slightly negative direction).
Mean PFS 3.64 on sotorasib vs 3.32 off; coef +0.322, p = 1.6e-53. Substantial marginal benefit.
coef -0.025, p = 0.25. No marginal effect.
coef +0.0036, p = 0.87. No marginal effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, albumin, LDH, weight loss, age, sex, smoking, histology, mutations, PDL1, TMB, and routine labs, treatment_pembrolizumab still shows a positive effect on pfs_months.
After adjustment, treatment_sotorasib still shows a positive marginal effect on pfs_months (i.e. the unadjusted effect is not entirely explained by patient mix).
After adjustment, treatment_olaparib still shows no overall effect.
After adjustment, treatment_osimertinib still shows no overall effect.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted pembrolizumab coefficient -0.006, p = 0.37. No detectable marginal benefit after adjustment.
Adjusted sotorasib coefficient +0.323, p ~ 0. Robust marginal benefit (likely concentrated in KRAS G12C subgroup, see iter 11).
Adjusted olaparib coefficient +0.003, p = 0.72. Confirmed null marginal effect.
Adjusted osimertinib coefficient -0.002, p = 0.83. Confirmed null marginal effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit (interaction coefficient) increases with higher pdl1_tps — i.e. the slope of pfs_months on treatment_pembrolizumab is larger when pdl1_tps is high.
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is greater in tmb_high=1 patients than tmb_high=0 patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient -0.116, p = 0.22. NOT significant; sign is opposite to the hypothesized direction (less benefit at higher TPS, not more).
Interaction coefficient -0.018, p = 0.68. NOT significant; sign also opposite to hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab is less effective (smaller or negative interaction coefficient) in stk11_mutation=1 patients than in stk11_mutation=0.
treatment_pembrolizumab is less effective in egfr_mutation=1 patients than in egfr_mutation=0 (clinical expectation: oncogene-addicted disease).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient -0.016, p = 0.77. No detectable STK11-modified pembrolizumab effect.

Interaction coefficient +0.099, p = 0.096. Direction is OPPOSITE to clinical expectation (slightly more pembro benefit in EGFR+, not less); not significant. After full adjustment, p = 0.38, fully null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months substantially more in patients with kras_g12c=1 than in kras_g12c=0 (large positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient +2.558, p ~ 0. Sotorasib main effect (in KRAS WT) is essentially null at -0.005 (p = 0.77); KRAS main effect (off sotorasib) is -0.121 (p = 0.001). Sotorasib confers ~+2.55 months of PFS only when kras_g12c=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months substantially more in patients with egfr_mutation=1 than in egfr_mutation=0 (positive interaction; expected clinically).
treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months substantially more in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than in brca2_mutation=0 (positive interaction; expected clinically).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient -0.020, p = 0.76. No osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutated patients in this cohort — directly contrary to the expected clinical pattern.
Interaction coefficient +0.037, p = 0.78. No olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutated patients — directly contrary to the expected clinical pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within kras_g12c=1 patients, treatment_sotorasib is associated with longer pfs_months than non-sotorasib (large positive subgroup mean difference).
Within kras_g12c=0 patients, treatment_sotorasib is NOT associated with longer pfs_months (null in the complement).
Within egfr_mutation=1 patients, treatment_osimertinib is associated with longer pfs_months than non-osimertinib.
Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months than non-olaparib.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test of pfs_months by treatment_sotorasib among kras_g12c==1.
analyses.result_summary: n_on=2246, n_off=4122. Mean PFS 5.77 on sotorasib vs 3.21 off, diff +2.55 months, p = 1.7e-202. Strongly significant.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test of pfs_months by treatment_sotorasib among kras_g12c==0.
analyses.result_summary: n_on=15298, n_off=28334. Mean PFS 3.329 on vs 3.334 off, diff -0.005 months, p = 0.80. Clean null.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test of pfs_months by treatment_osimertinib among egfr_mutation==1.
analyses.result_summary: n_on=1973, n_off=4544. Mean PFS 3.586 on vs 3.600 off, diff -0.014, p = 0.81. No detectable osimertinib effect within EGFR-mutant subgroup.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test of pfs_months by treatment_olaparib among brca2_mutation==1.
analyses.result_summary: n_on=417, n_off=1102. Mean PFS 3.32 on vs 3.31 off, diff +0.010, p = 0.93. No olaparib effect within BRCA2-mutant subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect is largest in pdl1_tps>=0.5 patients (subgroup mean comparison).
treatment_pembrolizumab effect is positive in tmb_high=1 patients but null in tmb_high=0.
treatment_pembrolizumab effect is most positive in patients without stk11_mutation (stk11=0).

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test by treatment_pembrolizumab within pdl1_tps>=0.5 vs <0.5.
analyses.result_summary: PDL1>=0.5: n_on=5342, n_off=5407, diff -0.070 (p=0.11). PDL1<0.5: diff -0.025 (p=0.26). No benefit in either subgroup; in PD-L1 high the point estimate is slightly NEGATIVE.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test by treatment_pembrolizumab within tmb_high=1 vs 0.
analyses.result_summary: TMB high: diff -0.048 (p=0.20). TMB low: diff -0.030 (p=0.20). No pembro benefit in either subgroup.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test by treatment_pembrolizumab within stk11=0 vs 1.
analyses.result_summary: STK11 negative: n_on=21281, diff -0.032, p = 0.14. STK11 positive: diff -0.049, p = 0.34. Both null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect differs across smoking_status — positive (or larger) in current/former smokers (smoking-induced TMB) versus never smokers.
treatment_pembrolizumab effect differs by histology — positive in squamous histology compared with adenocarcinoma.
treatment_pembrolizumab effect differs across ECOG strata — concentrated in ECOG 0/1 (fitter patients).

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test by treatment_pembrolizumab within each smoking stratum.
analyses.result_summary: Never: diff -0.008 (p=0.87). Former: diff -0.047 (p=0.077). Current: diff -0.029 (p=0.43). All null/slightly negative. No smoking-modified pembro benefit.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test by treatment_pembrolizumab within each histology.
analyses.result_summary: Adeno: diff -0.014 (p=0.55). Squamous: diff -0.074 (p=0.043) — note the only nominally 'significant' subgroup is in the WRONG direction (slightly worse PFS on pembro). No adjusted pembro × histology interaction (p=0.82).

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test by treatment_pembrolizumab within ECOG 0/1/2.
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0: diff +0.031 (p=0.35). ECOG 1: diff -0.066 (p=0.013). ECOG 2: diff -0.010 (p=0.82). Modest negative in ECOG 1; not coherent with hypothesis. After adjustment for albumin/weight-loss/stage/brain-mets, pembro × C(ecog_ps) interaction p ≥ 0.07 (NS).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND stk11_mutation=0, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months (combining the two most-cited positive predictors).
The triple interaction treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps × stk11_mutation is non-zero (i.e. STK11 modifies the PDL1-dependence of pembro effect).

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test by treatment_pembrolizumab in (pdl1>=0.5 & stk11=0).
analyses.result_summary: n_on=4552, n_off=4597. Mean PFS 3.40 on vs 3.50 off, diff -0.095, p = 0.044 — nominally significant in the WRONG direction (pembro slightly worse). Adjusted within-stratum coefficient -0.0004, p = 0.98 — fully null.
Triple interaction coefficient +0.085, p = 0.75. No higher-order modulation detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effect heterogeneity for treatment_pembrolizumab is most strongly driven by some feature other than PDL1/TMB/STK11 — i.e. an exhaustive screen will reveal an unexpected modifier.
Treatment effect heterogeneity for treatment_olaparib has its strongest interaction with brca2_mutation (verifying clinical expectation).
Treatment effect heterogeneity for treatment_osimertinib has its strongest interaction with egfr_mutation (verifying clinical expectation).

analyses.code: Loop over all 26 features running smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_pembrolizumab * f') and ranking interactions by p-value.
analyses.result_summary: Strongest unadjusted interaction is pembro × ecog (each ECOG level p ≈ 0.03–0.07, point estimates near 0); next strongest are pembro × egfr (p=0.10), pembro × pdl1 (p=0.22), pembro × kras_g12c (p~0.13). After full prognostic adjustment NO interaction reaches p<0.05 — strongest adjusted hit is pembro × kras_g12c (coef -0.064, p = 0.0065) but the magnitude is tiny and not corroborated by stratified means.

analyses.code: Loop over all features running smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib * f') and ranking interactions.
analyses.result_summary: No olaparib interaction is significant. Strongest adjusted hits: olaparib × sodium (p=0.058), olaparib × AST (p=0.079), olaparib × alkaline phosphatase (p=0.087). olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction p=0.78 — definitely not the strongest, refuting hypothesis.

analyses.code: Loop over all features running smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_osimertinib * f') and ranking interactions.
analyses.result_summary: Strongest osimertinib interactions are with brain mets (p=0.029), pdl1_tps (p=0.030), total bilirubin (p=0.032), sodium (p=0.042). osimertinib × egfr_mutation p=0.76 — not in the top hits, refuting hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sotorasib's KRAS-G12C-conditional benefit is itself modified by stk11_mutation — KRAS+STK11 co-mutation reduces the sotorasib benefit (positive triple interaction with opposite sign).
Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C+ patients is preserved across ECOG strata (does not vanish at ECOG 2 even though absolute PFS is shorter).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_sotorasib * kras_g12c * stk11_mutation', df).fit() and stratified subgroup tests.
analyses.result_summary: Triple interaction coefficient -0.27, p = 0.13 (NS). Stratified means: KRAS+STK11+ diff +2.33 (p=3.6e-25); KRAS+STK11- diff +2.59 (p=3.2e-179). Both subgroups derive substantial sotorasib benefit; STK11 status does not abolish it.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of treatment_sotorasib within kras_g12c==1 across ECOG levels.
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0 (n=2257): diff +2.47 (p=5.5e-70). ECOG 2 (n=954): diff +2.49 (p=2.8e-38). Sotorasib benefit is essentially independent of performance status within the KRAS G12C subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within egfr_mutation=1, treatment_osimertinib benefit is concentrated in patients with adenocarcinoma histology and never-smoker status (classical EGFR-mutant clinical phenotype).
Within egfr_mutation=1, treatment_osimertinib benefit is reduced in patients with brain metastases.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test of treatment_osimertinib within egfr_mutation=1 & histology=adenocarcinoma & smoking_status=never.
analyses.result_summary: n_on=1114, n_off=2507. Mean PFS 3.675 on vs 3.718 off, diff -0.043, p = 0.57. Even in the classical EGFR-mutant clinical phenotype, no osimertinib benefit detectable.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test of treatment_osimertinib within egfr_mutation=1 stratified by has_brain_mets.
analyses.result_summary: EGFR+, brain mets+: diff +0.051 (p=0.64). EGFR+, brain mets-: diff -0.029 (p=0.66). No benefit in either stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients without competing oncogenic drivers (egfr=0 & alk_fusion=0), treatment_olaparib has a positive effect on pfs_months.
Within brca2_mutation=1, treatment_olaparib effect is larger in fit (ecog_ps=0) patients than in poor-PS (ecog_ps=2) patients.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test of treatment_olaparib within brca2_mutation=1 & egfr_mutation=0 & alk_fusion=0.
analyses.result_summary: n_on=354, n_off=918. Mean PFS 3.374 on vs 3.297 off, diff +0.078, p = 0.57. No olaparib benefit even in 'cleaner' BRCA2 subset.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-tests of treatment_olaparib within brca2_mutation=1 & ecog_ps==0 vs ==2.
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0: diff -0.137 (p=0.52). ECOG 2: diff -0.189 (p=0.51). Both null with negative sign. Triple interaction olaparib × brca2 × ECOG also non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When all four treatments and their canonical biomarker interactions are entered jointly with full prognostic adjustment, only treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c remains significant (the other three biomarker-targeted interactions vanish).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_pembrolizumab * pdl1_tps + treatment_pembrolizumab * stk11_mutation + treatment_pembrolizumab * tmb_high + treatment_pembrolizumab * egfr_mutation + treatment_sotorasib * kras_g12c + treatment_osimertinib * egfr_mutation + treatment_olaparib * brca2_mutation + <full adjustment>', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c coef +2.533, p ~ 0. ALL OTHER interaction coefficients have |p|>0.2 (pembro:pdl1 p=0.94, pembro:stk11 p=0.88, pembro:tmb p=0.98, pembro:egfr p=0.68, osi:egfr p=0.23, ola:brca2 p=0.37). Joint model R² = 0.927, dominated by prognostic covariates (ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, age, sex, smoking, histology).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no joint subgroup of pdl1, tmb, stk11, and egfr in which treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months — i.e. an exhaustive small-feature subgroup search returns no positive treatment-effect cell for pembrolizumab.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-test of treatment_pembrolizumab in (pdl1_tps>=0.5 & tmb_high=1 & stk11_mutation=0 & egfr_mutation=0).
analyses.result_summary: n_on=1284, n_off=1249. Mean PFS 3.331 on vs 3.282 off, diff +0.049, p = 0.59. Even in the most clinically-favored pembrolizumab subgroup, no detectable benefit. Adjusted three-way interactions pembro × pdl1 × <feature> are all NS (best is pembro:pdl1:tmb_high coef +0.35, p=0.09).

proposed_hypotheses.text: TMB high is NOT prognostic after adjustment for histology, smoking and other covariates — its negative univariate association is confounded by histology/smoking distribution.
Age is positively associated with pfs_months even after adjustment (the inverted age-PFS relationship is not an artifact of confounding by other features).

analyses.code: Adjusted main-effect coefficient for tmb_high in joint model.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted tmb_high coefficient -0.006, p = 0.46. The negative univariate effect (-0.190, p=9e-18) collapses after adjustment — confounded.

analyses.code: Adjusted main-effect coefficient for age_years in joint model.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age_years coefficient +0.168 months/year, p ~ 0 (t≈614). Even with full prognostic adjustment, older age is associated with longer PFS in this synthetic cohort — likely a feature of the data-generating process rather than clinical reality.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for sotorasib: treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by approximately 2.5 months only in patients with kras_g12c=1; the effect is NOT further restricted by stk11_mutation status, ecog_ps, sex, smoking_status, or histology.

Final treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for pembrolizumab: there is no subgroup definable from the available features in which treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months meaningfully — the effect is null overall and in every clinically-motivated subgroup tested.

Final treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for osimertinib: treatment_osimertinib has NO measurable effect on pfs_months in this dataset — including in patients with egfr_mutation=1, regardless of histology, smoking_status, brain metastases, or ecog_ps.

Final treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for olaparib: treatment_olaparib has NO measurable effect on pfs_months in this dataset — including in patients with brca2_mutation=1, regardless of histology, smoking, or PS strata.

analyses.code: Adjusted within-stratum models. Restricted to kras_g12c=1 vs kras_g12c=0 and to additional partitions by STK11/ECOG/sex/histology.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient of treatment_sotorasib within kras_g12c=1: +2.529, p ~ 0. Within kras_g12c=0: +0.001, p = 0.66. Stratified by ECOG (0 vs 2) the within-KRAS+ effect is +2.47 and +2.49 respectively. STK11 stratification within KRAS+ gives +2.59 (STK11-) and +2.33 (STK11+). Effect is robust across these splits, so the predictive subgroup is best defined as simply kras_g12c=1.

analyses.code: Final pembrolizumab subgroup screen including pdl1_tps>=0.5 & stk11_mutation=0 & egfr_mutation=0 & tmb_high=1.
analyses.result_summary: Best clinically-favored joint subgroup (pdl1>=0.5 & stk11=0 & egfr=0 & tmb_high=1): n_on=1284, n_off=1249, diff +0.049 (p=0.59). Pembrolizumab × every individual feature interaction NS after adjustment. Conclusion: no detectable pembrolizumab benefit subgroup.

analyses.code: Adjusted within-stratum models for treatment_osimertinib in egfr_mutation=1 and clinically-canonical subgroups.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment_osimertinib within EGFR+: coef +0.011, p = 0.43. Within EGFR+ & adeno & never-smoker: +0.043 against (diff -0.043, p=0.57). Within EGFR+ & no brain mets: -0.029 (p=0.66). All null.

analyses.code: Adjusted within-stratum models for treatment_olaparib in brca2_mutation=1 and clinically-canonical subgroups.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib × brca2 interaction p = 0.97. Subgroup means: brca2=1 (any) diff +0.010 (p=0.93); brca2=1 & no other driver +0.078 (p=0.57); brca2=1 & ecog=0 -0.137 (p=0.52); brca2=1 & ecog=2 -0.189 (p=0.51). All null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Overall conclusion: the strongest predictors of pfs_months in this dataset are ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, age_years, smoking_status, and histology. Among the four treatments, only treatment_sotorasib shows efficacy, restricted to the kras_g12c=1 subgroup (~+2.5 months PFS); treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_osimertinib, and treatment_olaparib show no measurable PFS benefit overall or in any tested subgroup.

analyses.code: Final joint OLS: pfs_months ~ treatment_sotorasib*kras_g12c + treatment_pembrolizumab + treatment_olaparib + treatment_osimertinib + <every prognostic covariate>.
analyses.result_summary: Joint model (R² = 0.927). Significant predictors (|t|>3): ecog_ps levels, stage_iv (-1.41), has_brain_mets (-0.91), albumin_g_dl (+0.46), ldh_u_l (-5e-4), weight_loss_pct_6mo (-0.073), age_years (+0.168), sex_female (-0.21), smoking former (+0.55) and never (+0.55) vs current, histology squamous (-0.73), brca2_mutation (-0.11), alk_fusion (-0.027), and treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c (+2.53). All four treatment main effects are essentially zero (|coef|<0.01, p>0.3). The dataset thus shows ONE clinically-coherent predictive biomarker–treatment pair (KRAS G12C → sotorasib) and three apparent treatment 'failures' (pembrolizumab, osimertinib, olaparib) that may reflect the data-generating process rather than real-world clinical response patterns.
